hero.question: Houston Astros vs. Colorado Rockies
hero.prob_take:
At 12.5%, the market is essentially calling this a 1-in-8 shot, which means bettors have moved past "unlikely" straight into "basically won't happen." Colorado would need a perfect storm of Astros collapse and Rockies execution that the market has decided isn't worth pricing in seriously.

The real question is whether this is laziness or accuracy: the Astros are genuinely dominant or the Rockies catch enough breaks that 12.5% undercounts coin-flip variance in a single game, which historically these markets tend to do with heavy favorites.

daily_take.headline: Astros collapse to 12.5% signals sharp money fleeing Houston
daily_take.deck: The Astros just tanked 52 points in 24 hours on what looks like routine baseball odds. That's not noise. Sharp money doesn't move half a point on random variance. Either new injury data hit the books, or the market repriced Houston's actual strength versus what casual bettors thought. At 12.5%, you're getting 8-to-1 odds on a team that probably shouldn't be longer than 6-to-1. The play depends on your read of why it moved. If it's new information, respect it. If it's late-night Reddit recency bias about one bad game, buy the dip.

sidebar.headline: Bondi at 88.5% looks like a crowded conviction trade. Tiny volume, massive odds. Be careful fighting a consensus this lopsided.
